title: Define acceptance policy for optimization loops
description:
## Summary
Define the accept/reject policy for score-optimization loops so "did the needle move?" becomes an explicit, auditable rule.

## Deliverables
- Specify how deterministic reference runs and random-sample runs combine into one decision.
- Define clear acceptance/rejection criteria for iterative score changes.
- Keep the policy stable and reviewable across optimization cycles.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: codify optimization acceptance decision function combining deterministic reference evidence + random-sample generalization evidence, with explicit accept/reject/inconclusive outputs and rationale.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented explicit optimization-loop acceptance policy.

Added module:
- plexus/cli/shared/optimization_acceptance_policy.py

Policy behavior:
- Combines deterministic reference evaluation evidence and random-sample generalization evidence.
- Uses value(E) from evaluation_value_function as quantitative basis.
- Decision outputs are explicit and auditable:
  - accept
  - reject (insufficient_reference_improvement)
  - reject (generalization_regression)
  - inconclusive (insufficient_evidence)
- Configurable thresholds:
  - minimum required reference improvement
  - maximum allowed random/generalization drop

Tests:
- plexus/cli/shared/optimization_acceptance_policy_test.py
- pytest result: 4 passed.
